title: Stellan Skarsgård Wins Golden Globe for Best Supporting Actor 'Sentimental Value,' Says 'Cinema Should Be Seen in Cinemas'
text: Swedish actor Stellan Skarsgård won the Golden Globe for Performance by a Male Actor in a Supporting Film Role at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes ceremony on January 11, 2026, for his work in the Norwegian film Sentimental Value. In his acceptance speech, Skarsgård expressed gratitude to his wife Megan Everett-Skarsgård and his children, joking about playing a bad father in the film. He highlighted the achievement, noting: a small Norwegian film with no money for advertising or anything that gets to see the world in this way. The actor used the platform to advocate for theatrical cinema, stating that Cinema should be seen in cinemas, describing the moviegoing experience as magical where the lights go down and eventually you share the pulse with some other people. Skarsgård's winning performance was in director Joachim Trier's film, which also features Renate Reinsve and Elle Fanning and centers on a filmmaker navigating his relationship with his daughters while preparing a production. He previously won a Golden Globe for the television miniseries Chernobyl and was nominated at the European Film Awards for best European actor.
